claims.claimText: BMC operates a Child Neurology Residency Program.

claims.quote: Interview Day 2025-2026 - All Virtual

claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Residency interview format — Tier 2 residency program info.

claims.quote:
Rinat Jonas, MD
Director – Child Neurology Residency Program
claims.claimText: Program director is Rinat Jonas, MD.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Residency leadership contact — Tier 2.

claims.quote:
Patrick Mabray, MD, PhD
Associate Director – Child Neurology Residency Program
claims.claimText: Associate director is Patrick Mabray, MD, PhD.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Residency leadership — Tier 2.

claims.quote: we collaborate with Boston Children’s Hospital and other hospitals in the area to provide a wide variety of elective opportunities in all subspecialties of child neurology.
claims.claimText: Program collaborates with Boston Children's Hospital for elective opportunities in child neurology subspecialties.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Electives described are within residency training, not visiting medical student electives.

claims.quote: Our three-year pediatric neurology residency training program is designed to:
claims.claimText: BMC offers a three-year pediatric neurology residency training program.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Page describes a pediatric neurology residency training program — GME content, Tier 2.

claims.quote: The child neurology residency program offers two types of positions: a categorical track and a reserved track.
claims.claimText: Child neurology residency offers categorical and reserved tracks.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Two residency position tracks described — Tier 2 GME content.

claims.quote: This track includes two years of training in Pediatrics in the Boston Combined Residency Program (BCRP) program at Boston Medical Center and Boston Children’s Hospital, and three years of pediatric neurology training at Boston Medical Center.
claims.claimText: Categorical track: 2 yrs pediatrics in BCRP plus 3 yrs pediatric neurology at BMC.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Describes residency training structure (categorical track) — Tier 2.

claims.quote: This track is offered to candidates who have completed at least two years of pediatrics training in an ACGME-accredited program and is prepared to begin in July of the academic year, four months after completion of the match.
claims.claimText: Reserved track requires ≥2 yrs pediatrics in an ACGME-accredited program.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Eligibility for residency reserved track — Tier 2.

claims.quote: We require an interview, a personal statement, and three letters of reference for each track.
claims.claimText: Application requires interview, personal statement, and three letters of reference.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Application requirements for the residency — Tier 2.

claims.quote: A distinguishing feature of our program is our close collaboration with other medical institutions in Boston, with core and elective rotations at Boston Children's Hospital, Tufts Medical Center, Massachusetts General Hospital, and Spaulding rehabilitation center.
claims.claimText: Core and elective rotations at affiliated Boston institutions during residency.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Describes residency rotation sites — Tier 2 trainee content.

futureLaneSignals.quote: All application materials should be submitted through the Electronic Residency Application Service (ERAS®) system. We will not review materials sent by email.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Applications must be submitted through ERAS; email submissions not accepted.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Residency application route via ERAS — Tier 2 content.

futureLaneSignals.quote: USMLE Step 1 is required in order to be considered for an interview. While USMLE Step 2CK scores are not required for application, they can be helpful. There is no specific "cut-off" score for USMLE exams.

futureLaneSignals.quote: Graduation from medical school or pediatrics residency within the past ten years.

futureLaneSignals.quote:
What visas do you accept for International Medical Graduates?
J-1.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: Program accepts J-1 visa for International Medical Graduates.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: Visa sponsorship statement for residency applicants — Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.quote:
Do you accept International Medical Graduates?
Yes. However, you also must have:
An ECFMG certificate
Have passed the USMLE 1 and 2
Have completed 2 years in an ACGME accredited Pediatrics training program before entering our program.
futureLaneSignals.claimText: IMGs accepted with ECFMG certificate, USMLE 1 and 2 passes, and 2 years ACGME pediatrics training.
futureLaneSignals.tierAssignmentRationale: IMG eligibility for residency — Tier 2.

futureLaneSignals.quote: If in the United States on a visa, you must be eligible to remain for an additional three years in order to complete our training program.

claims.quote: Fourth year medical students rotate at the Boston Medical Center. This sub-internship (sub-I) is offered early in the fourth year and is designed for students interested in a career in Otolaryngology (ENT).
claims.claimText: BMC ENT department offers a sub-internship for fourth-year medical students interested in Otolaryngology, offered early in MS4.
claims.limitations: Department-level page; eligibility audience (outside vs affiliated MS4s) not explicitly stated.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Sub-internship for fourth-year medical students is a pre-residency USCE Tier 1 opportunity.

claims.quote: is designed for students interested in a career in Otolaryngology (ENT). Although it is not required, applicants who are considering Boston Medical Center for a residency spot in Otolaryngology (ENT) are encouraged to rotate with us.
claims.claimText: Sub-I is designed for students interested in ENT careers; rotation encouraged but not required for BMC ENT residency applicants.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Audience/eligibility statement about Tier 1 sub-I.
